title: Get a one-time payment of $120 in summer food benefits for your child
body:
Each eligible child can receive a *one-time payment of $120*. Families may use the money to pay for groceries during the summer months. 

Some eligible families are automatically enrolled, and some need to submit an application. We can recommend whether you should apply for your child. Tap "Do I need to apply?" and enter your child's information.
accordionTitle: How do I know if my child can get Summer EBT?
body2: Children may be automatically enrolled if we have information to confirm their eligibility. This usually happens for one of the following reasons:
body3:
They are between ages 5 and 18. And they get SNAP (food stamps), TANF (cash assistance), Medicaid, or the Food Distribution Program on Indian Reservations (FDPIR)
Or they are temporarily unhoused or in foster care
Or they get free or low-cost meals at school
Or they are enrolled in a school that participates in the National School Lunch Program (NSLP)
body4: But, children who are not automatically enrolled may still be able to get the benefit. Parents or guardians should fill out an application to see if they qualify. Tap ["Do I need to apply?"](link) to see if you need to apply for your child.
